definition: The action or process of innovating; a new method, idea, product, etc.
example: Solving big problems is easier than solving little problems.

definition: The ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills.
example: We simply do not know what the limit to intelligence is.
source: Comments on AI and future potential

definition: An important assignment carried out for political, religious, or commercial purposes.
example: The perfect search engine would understand exactly what you mean and give back exactly what you want.
source: Early Google vision / IPO letter era

example: Don't be evil. Do the right thing. Build products that make the world better.
speech: Don't Be Evil / Do the Right Thing

definition: To suppress or restrain (an action or quality).
A point or level beyond which something does not or may not extend or pass.
example: We simply do not know what the limit to intelligence is.
source: Comments on AI and future potential

definition: A scientific procedure undertaken to make a discovery, test a hypothesis, or demonstrate a known fact.
example: We do lots of stuff. The only way you are going to have success is to have lots of failures.

definition: The action of one object coming forcibly into contact with another; a marked effect or influence.
example: It's not enough not to be evil. We also actively try to be good.
speech: Don't Be Evil / Do the Right Thing
